lines: +    it("shuts down promptly when an idle $search cursor holds an executor reference", function () {
+                // Use a long timeout (not infinite) so the test fails fast with a clear message if
+                // the idle cursor is not disposed promptly, rather than hanging until the timeout.
+        // mongot returns all results in one batch with cursor id 0 (exhausted on mongot). mongod
+        // still keeps a client-visible cursor open because of the small batchSize below, and the
+        // pipeline (and its TaskExecutorCursor, which pins the mongot executor) stays alive for that
+        // cursorId is just the (non-zero) key the mock stores this history under. The response's
+        // own cursor.id is 0 (mongot is exhausted), so mongod sends neither a getMore nor a
+        // Open a $search cursor and read only the first document, leaving the cursor idle on mongod.
+        // The cursor is still open (not exhausted) and thus still pinning the mongot executor.
+        // Stop mongod. The executor wait is set long enough that the only thing that makes shutdown
+        // prompt is disposing the idle cursor that holds the executor reference.
+        assert.eq(exitCode, MongoRunner.EXIT_CLEAN, "mongod did not shut down cleanly");
+        // Clean shutdown should be prompt; the 10s executor timeout is a backstop that should never
+        // fire if the idle cursor is disposed correctly.
+        assert.lt(elapsedMs, 3000, "mongod shutdown was slow, indicating it hung on the executor");
// Inform Flow Control to stop gating writes on ticket admission. This must be done before the
// Periodic Runner is shut down (see SERVER-41751).
+        // Dispose idle cursors so they release their references to the search task executors before
+                // Pinned by an in-progress operation. That operation has already been interrupted
+                // by setKillAllOperations(); skip the cursor and let the operation clean it up as
+                // it unwinds, otherwise we would dispose a cursor that is still in use.
+            // Advance the iterator first since erasing from the lockedPartition will invalidate any
+    // The partition lock is released before disposing of any cursors.
+     * Disposes of every idle (non-pinned) cursor registered with this manager, returning the number
+     * disposed. Intended to be called once during shutdown (after operations are killed) so cursors
+     * release the resources they hold - notably the references $search cursors keep to the mongot
+     * task executors. Cursors still pinned by an in-progress operation are skipped; those
+     * operations have already been interrupted by setKillAllOperations() and will clean up their
* Kills cursors in this cursor manager with matching logical sessions. Returns a pair with the
* overall Status of the operation and the number of cursors successfully killed.
+ * Test that killAllCursorsForShutdown disposes of all idle cursors. This is what releases the
+ * references that $search/$vectorSearch cursors hold to the search task executors during shutdown.
+        // Letting the returned pin go out of scope leaves an idle (non-pinned) cursor registered.
+    // Safe to call again with nothing registered.
+ * Test that killAllCursorsForShutdown does not dispose of a cursor that is still pinned by an
+ * in-progress operation; such an operation has already been interrupted and will clean up its own
+    // The cursor is pinned, so it must be skipped and remain registered.
+ * Test that killAllCursorsForShutdown disposes idle cursors while leaving pinned ones in place.
+    // Only the two idle cursors are disposed; the pinned cursor is skipped.
* Test that pinned cursors do not get timed out.
+            Maximum time, in milliseconds, that server shutdown will wait for outstanding references
+            to the mongot and search-index-management task executors to be released before
+            proceeding anyway. Outstanding references may be held by in-progress or idle
+            $search/$vectorSearch/$searchMeta cursors. A value of 0 means wait forever, which can
description: Configures whether or not shards should communicate with mongot using gRPC.
// Tick the log sampler in advance to avoid logging immediately - only log if it's a long wait.
+    // Bound how long we wait for outstanding references (held by in-progress or idle search
+    // cursors) to be released; an unbounded wait can hang shutdown. If they outlive the timeout,
+    // proceed and let the executor be reclaimed once its remaining owners go away. A value of 0
+                          "Timed out waiting for the search task executor to be destroyed during "
+                          "shutdown; proceeding anyway. Outstanding references will be released "
+                          "when their owning operations or cursors complete.",
LOGV2_INFO(11323800, "Waiting for search task executor to be destroyed.");

+// Verifies that server shutdown does not hang indefinitely when something still holds a reference
+// to the mongot task executor - which is exactly what an in-progress or idle $search cursor does.
+// The original implementation waited unbounded for the reference to drop, blocking shutdown for
+// tens of minutes until Atlas Automation SIGKILLed the process.
+        // A non-empty host makes shutdownSearchExecutorsIfNeeded() exercise the mongot executor
+        // shutdown/destroy path. The address is never connected to in this test.
+    // Simulate an idle $search cursor that still holds a strong reference to the mongot task
+    // executor across shutdown. This reference would never be released by the time shutdown reaches
+    // this point, so an unbounded wait hangs forever.
+    // Keep the bounded wait short so the test is fast.
+        // Generous bound relative to the 500ms timeout; the fix returns in ~0.5s, the bug never
+    // Always release the reference and join the thread before asserting, so the test never leaks a
+    // running thread (even when the assertion below fails on buggy, unbounded-wait code).
+        << "shutdownSearchExecutorsIfNeeded() blocked past its timeout while a reference to the "
